name: agent-cost-mcp
tagline: Local-first Claude Code cost analyzer. Zero cloud. No API key.
description: Local-first MCP server that parses Claude Code JSONL session logs to surface per-tool spend, daily trends, and optimization hints.
triggerPhrases: How much did my last Claude Code session cost?
Which tools are eating my context window?
Why is Claude Code expensive this week?
Show me the cost trend for the last 7 days.
What should I disable to save tokens?
Are my cache reads actually saving me money?
Can I get a per-tool cost breakdown without Datadog?

tools.name: get_session_cost
tools.description: Parse a single Claude Code session and return token totals (input, output, cache-read, cache-creation), turn count, and estimated USD cost.

tools.name: get_tool_usage
tools.description: Aggregate tool invocations across one session or a filtered project log directory, reporting per-tool call counts and context-share percentages.

tools.name: get_cost_trend
tools.description: Roll session logs into a day-by-day cost trend for a local project path, with per-day sessions, tokens, and estimated spend.

tools.name: suggest_optimizations
tools.description: Generate lightweight optimization suggestions (cache-read ratios, abandoned tool calls, heaviest turns) from a parsed session log.

claude-code.command: claude mcp add --transport stdio agent-cost -- npx -y @vk0/agent-cost-mcp
